item_name: Spline / 3D for Apps — When 3D Is Worth the Cost
definition_origin: 3D-for-app-design refers to using browser-renderable or runtime-renderable 3D scenes (glTF/GLB, USDZ, Spline scenes, Three.js/R3F components) as hero illustration, brand moments, or product surfaces in apps. Emerged ~2020 with Spline democratizing the toolchain; matured through 2023-2026 with AI-assisted modelers (Bezi, Spline AI, Meshy, Luma) reducing the entry skill cost.
core_purpose: Communicates depth, materiality, and physicality that 2D cannot — premium leather, glass refraction, motion-parallax depth — registering at the Visceral level (instant 'this feels expensive/futuristic'). Behavioral when 3D is interactive (hover-rotate product, drag-to-explore architecture). Reflective when the 3D scene becomes the brand mnemonic (Stripe's iridescent flow, Linear's prism, Apple Vision Pro hero). 3D's emotional job is signaling tier — the visceral promise of premium-craft.
when_needed: Worth it: hero landing pages (B2B SaaS marketing), AI-product surfaces where the AI itself needs a metaphor (orb, prism, particle field), distinctive brand moments (404 page, onboarding finale, paywall), AR product preview (USDZ on iOS for furniture/apparel/glasses), data-viz where dimensionality has meaning (architecture, logistics, scientific). Not worth it: in-app micro-interactions (Lottie/Rive cheaper, lighter), generic illustration headers (2D vector wins), small icons (3D wastes payload), if you can't art-direct (becomes slop fast). Consumer mobile: rare; AI-wrapper: signature use; B2B/dev-tool: marketing only; marketing site: increasingly default for premium tiers.
format_options: glTF/GLB (.gltf/.glb): web standard, JSON+binary, supported by Three.js/R3F/Babylon/Model-viewer. GLB is the binary single-file variant — preferred for shipping. Typical 100KB-2MB for stylized assets, 5-50MB for hero scenes. USDZ (.usdz): Apple's AR Quick Look format, iOS-native, drops directly into Safari/iMessage with one tap to AR. Convert from glTF via Apple Reality Converter. FBX/OBJ (.fbx/.obj): legacy DCC interchange — avoid shipping, use only for tool round-trip. Spline scene URL: hosted .splinecode runtime via @splinetool/react-spline (saves you from R3F setup, costs you Spline-runtime KB). React Three Fiber components (.tsx generated from gltfjsx): full code-control export — best for production. Lottie 3D: Spline can export simplified 3D motion to Lottie for low-bandwidth fallback. WebGL Canvas screenshots/videos: WebM/MP4 fallback for when WebGL is disabled.
file_size_performance: Target ≤500 KB total scene payload for mobile hero (compressed GLB with Draco mesh compression + KTX2 textures). Use gltf-transform CLI: 'gltf-transform optimize input.glb output.glb' cuts 50-90%. Texture budget: 1024x1024 max for mobile, KTX2/BasisU compressed. Polygon budget: <50k tris for mobile, <200k for desktop hero. Defer init: lazy-load Spline/R3F bundle only when in viewport (IntersectionObserver). Use OrbitControls.enableDamping=true sparingly. Test on 4x CPU throttle.
free_tools: Spline (free tier: unlimited public projects, 1 editor seat, exports to code/glTF/USDZ/MP4 with watermark on hosted exports, Spline AI 3D-generate has limited free credits, web-based no-install). Bezi (free-forever tier: collaborative 3D prototyping, AI-assisted, exports to glTF/USDZ, paid plans from $80/mo for teams — note Bezi pivoted toward Unity AI assist in 2026). Blender (free, FOSS, professional-tier — steepest learning curve, Cycles + Eevee renderers, native USDZ export since 4.0, Geometry Nodes for procedural, ~500hr to mastery). Meshy (free tier: 200 credits/mo, text-to-3D + image-to-3D + remesh, exports glTF/FBX/USDZ, ideal for prop generation). Luma AI (free Genie 1.0 text-to-3D, Luma Dream Machine for video reference). RunwayML (limited free tier, image-to-3D experimental). Sketchfab (free hosting + viewer, embed via iframe, free model library under CC). Polycam (free tier: scan real objects via iPhone LiDAR, export glTF/USDZ). Adobe Substance 3D Sampler (free trial — for material capture). gltfjsx (free CLI, convert GLB→React Three Fiber JSX). Three.js Editor (free, browser-based scene assembly).
paid_tools: Spline Starter ($12/seat/mo annual, $15/mo monthly): unlimited private files, no watermark, brand kit, version history. Worth it once you ship 3D in production. Spline Professional ($20/seat/mo annual): advanced physics, code-export pro features. Cinema 4D ($59.91/mo or $719/yr): industry-standard hero motion-design 3D — overkill for most app dev. Adobe Substance 3D Collection ($49.99/mo): texturing/material libraries, only when you need PBR-pro materials. Maxon Redshift / Octane: pro renderers — only if you're producing baked render videos. Tiering: starter $12-15/mo (Spline Starter) — sufficient for most consumer/B2B apps shipping a single hero scene. Pro $20-30/mo (Spline Pro) — for ongoing 3D practice. Enterprise $50+/mo (Cinema 4D + Substance) — only for video/marketing-heavy product teams.
gemini_pro_workflow: Step 1 — Imagen 4 / Whisk for concept-art reference: 'isometric 3D scene of a glowing crystal orb on a soft pastel pedestal, soft global illumination, premium product render, behance trending, octane render aesthetic'. Use as moodboard, NOT as the actual 3D source. Step 2 — Gemini Pro multimodal: paste Imagen output + ask 'Generate a Spline-compatible scene description with named primitives, transforms, materials, and lighting setup that recreates this image'. Step 3 — Iterate inside Spline using its built-in Spline AI ('add a glowing toroid above the orb', 'soften the shadow'). Step 4 — Veo for motion reference videos when you need to communicate the desired camera move or scene animation to yourself before keyframing. Step 5 — Use Gemini's code-mode to generate the R3F wrapper component once you've exported the GLB, and to write the Draco/KTX2 optimization script. Custom Gem: '3D Scene Director' with system prompt 'You are a 3D art director specializing in app brand-moment scenes. Given a brand archetype and a feature, output: scene composition (3-5 elements + transforms), materials list, lighting (3-point or HDRI suggestion), camera FOV, and a 4-second animation timeline'.
workflow_steps: 1. Decide intent (15 min): is this a hero brand moment, AR product preview, or interactive widget? If unclear, kill and use 2D. 2. Reference moodboard (30 min): collect 8-12 reference images via Imagen + Pinterest; lock visual direction. 3. Block scene in Spline (60-90 min): primitive shapes, layout composition, single key light; aim for silhouette readability before any material work. 4. Materials pass (45 min): apply PBR materials, HDRI environment, refine roughness/metalness; avoid generic glassmorphic blob. 5. Lighting pass (30 min): three-point or HDRI + accent rim; render at multiple angles to confirm 3D works at hero size + thumbnail. 6. Animate (45 min if needed): Spline timeline or state machine — gentle camera orbit, single hero rotation, subtle hover-on-input. 7. Optimize (30 min): Draco mesh compression, KTX2 textures, target ≤500 KB. 8. Export paths: GLB for web (R3F), USDZ for iOS AR, code-export for tightest integration. 9. Integrate (30-60 min): R3F wrapper component, Suspense lazy-load, IntersectionObserver gate, fallback poster image. 10. Test reduced-motion + perf on low-end Android. Total first scene: 6-10 hr. Subsequent: 2-3 hr.
ai_prompt_templates: Spline AI scene prompt: 'A {archetype_descriptor} 3D scene featuring {primary_object}, on a {background} backdrop, lit with {lighting_setup}, materials: {primary_material} primary, {secondary_material} accent, color palette {hex1} {hex2} {hex3}, composition centered with {focal_point}, no glassmorphism, no generic isometric voxels, distinctive {asymmetry_detail}.' Imagen reference prompt: 'Studio product shot of {object}, {material}, soft three-point lighting, {brand_palette}, premium editorial render, octane-quality global illumination, behance trending, no abstract gradient blobs, distinctive composition with {detail}, isolated on {background}.' Gemini critique prompt: 'Review this 3D scene for slop signs: generic isometric voxel cluster, glassy abstract blob, default HDRI lighting, sky-blue gradient backdrop, kawaii squircle without character. List specific revisions with material/light/composition fixes.' R3F integration prompt: 'Given this exported GLB, write a TypeScript R3F component with Draco loader, KTX2 textures, IntersectionObserver lazy-load, fallback poster image, and prefers-reduced-motion gate.'
claude_code_integration: Install: pnpm add @react-three/fiber three @react-three/drei @splinetool/react-spline; for optimization: pnpm add -D @gltf-transform/cli. Drop GLB assets in apps/web/public/3d/ for web, apps/mobile/src/assets/3d/ for mobile (note: RN 3D requires expo-three or react-native-skia). Claude Code can: (1) generate the gltfjsx-converted React component with proper typing; (2) write optimize-3d.mjs build script (Draco + KTX2 pipeline); (3) generate Suspense + ErrorBoundary + reduced-motion poster wrappers; (4) emit USDZ conversion script via Apple Reality Converter CLI; (5) configure Vite/Metro to handle .glb/.usdz as static assets; (6) write Storybook stories with multiple camera angles. For Spline: '<Spline scene="https://prod.spline.design/.../scene.splinecode" />' but consider self-hosting the .splinecode file to avoid Spline CDN dependency.
emotional_design_integration: Caregiver: soft, organic shapes, pastel materials, slow ambient motion (Calm/Headspace would use a breathing-pulse 3D orb). Magician: iridescent gradients, particle systems, refractive glass, transformative motion (Linear's prism, OpenAI's Sora-style scenes). Jester: bouncy bright primitives, exaggerated rubber-band camera moves, mascot-as-3D-character (Discord, Slack-mascot territory). Sage: minimal, precise architectural geometry, neutral materials, subtle motion (Stripe's hero animations, Notion's calligraphy curves). Hero: bold metallic surfaces, dramatic rim lighting, triumphant reveal (Nike, Strava, Apple Vision Pro). Outlaw: gritty textures, neon, broken-glass fragments. Note: 3D becomes slop the moment archetype isn't enforced — generic 'pastel iso cube on pastel backdrop' reads as no-archetype.
specific_examples: Stripe (https://stripe.com) — flowing iridescent ribbon hero, signature Magician-archetype mark, set the 2022-2026 web-3D bar. Linear (https://linear.app) — prism mark + tasteful 3D hero on marketing pages, Sage-craft archetype. Apple Vision Pro landing — USDZ hero scenes inline-rendered via Quick Look, masterclass in tasteful 3D. Arc Browser (https://arc.net, archived but referenced) — playful 3D mascot moments, Jester archetype. Vercel (https://vercel.com) — particle/wireframe globe representing edge network. Rive (https://rive.app) — animated 3D mark on marketing. Bezi (https://bezi.com) — uses its own product to render 3D demos. Spatial.io — entire 3D product surface. Liveblocks marketing — playful 3D component motion. Counter-examples to avoid: every 'AI startup with a glassy gradient blob' from 2023 — homogeneous slop.
anti_ai_slop_traps: (1) Generic isometric voxel scenes (Minecraft-but-pastel cityscapes) — instantly recognizable as 2023 startup template; counter: replace iso with custom camera angle + asymmetric composition. (2) Glassy abstract gradient blobs floating in sky-blue backdrop — peak Spline-AI default; counter: replace with branded geometry + custom HDRI + editorial composition. (3) Default Spline 'pastel sphere on pastel backdrop' — 100k startups have this; counter: introduce a distinctive prop, asymmetric lighting, signature material. (4) Kawaii 3D mascot with melted hands or cylindrical chibi body — image-to-3D AI tells; counter: model the silhouette manually, use AI only for textures. (5) HDR sky environment as default — every Spline-AI output uses studio_small_03.hdr; counter: import a custom HDRI or build a 3-light setup. (6) PBR over-shine — everything looks like wet plastic; counter: roughness ≥0.5 unless you want it wet. (7) Cinema 4D 'Octane preset' look identical across 50 portfolios. Counter-prompts: 'NO generic isometric voxel cluster, NO glassy gradient blob, NO kawaii squircle mascot, NO sky-blue pastel backdrop, NO studio_small_03 HDRI, distinctive {custom_detail}, asymmetric composition, editorial product-render style.'
common_pitfalls: Performance: shipping a 5MB GLB on a marketing landing crushes Lighthouse — always run gltf-transform optimize. CLS (cumulative layout shift): 3D viewport pops in late and shifts page — reserve aspect-ratio space + show poster image during load. Battery: continuously animating WebGL scenes drain mobile battery — pause when offscreen via IntersectionObserver. Accessibility: 3D motion ignored by reduced-motion preferences by default — wire up window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)') and serve poster image. Scope creep: solo dev spends 40 hours on a hero scene that's seen for 2 seconds — set 8-hr cap on first 3D scene. Tool lock-in: hosted Spline scenes break if Spline changes pricing — always export GLB as backup. Asset version control: GLBs are binary and bloat git — use git LFS or external CDN. SEO: 3D content is invisible to crawlers — ensure semantic <h1>/copy is independent of the 3D layer.
accessibility_notes: Provide alt-text equivalent: aria-label on the 3D container ('Animated 3D scene of a glowing prism, decorative'). Honor prefers-reduced-motion: serve a high-res poster image instead of WebGL. Ensure color-not-only-signal: any data 3D viz needs a 2D legend. Keyboard navigation: focusable 3D models should respond to arrow keys for rotate. For AR Quick Look (USDZ), include captions if motion is meaningful. Screen-reader: mark decorative 3D as aria-hidden=true.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile (RN/iOS/Android): 3D requires expo-three, react-native-skia, or react-three/native — heavier setup, smaller payload tolerance (≤300KB GLB), prefer USDZ for iOS AR Quick Look. Native iOS: USDZ + ARKit gives best fidelity, no WebGL involved. Web: full R3F/Three.js power, lazy-load via dynamic import, can ship larger payloads on desktop, must handle WebGL2 fallback for old devices. Marketing site web: aggressive use OK; in-app mobile: use 3D sparingly because of battery/thermal.
sources: [Spline 3D Design](https://spline.design/); [Spline AI 3D Generation docs](https://docs.spline.design/spline-ai/ai-3d-generation); [r3f-spline GitHub hook](https://github.com/splinetool/r3f-spline); [Spline vs r3f Medium comparison](https://medium.com/@akbar123jason/simplifying-3d-integration-in-react-spline-vs-react-three-fiber-3f5e1a9e39d3); [Bezi pricing 2026](https://www.bezi.com/pricing); [Bezi review SaaSworthy](https://www.saasworthy.com/product/bezi); [Blender 2026 roadmap](https://www.blender.org/development/projects-to-look-forward-to-in-2026/); [Blender mobile/iPad status](https://www.macrumors.com/2026/02/27/blender-ipad-pro-app-development-halted/); [Free 3D software guide 2026](https://www.seeles.ai/resources/blogs/free-3d-model-design-software); [Needle vs R3F vs Spline](https://cloud.needle.tools/compare/needle-vs-r3f-vs-spline)
